paragraphs: Hi, I'm Vidubha Sankha — a fresh data analyst and BICT (Honours) graduate from the University of Vavuniya, passionate about turning raw data into clear, actionable insights.

I work with SQL, Python, Excel, and Tableau/Power BI to explore, clean, and visualize data, and I bring a solid foundation in statistics to make sure my findings are grounded in evidence, not just patterns.

projects.title: AI & Machine Learning Powered Road Damage Detection System for Sri Lanka

tabs.problem:
Sri Lankan road networks suffer from undocumented potholes, structural cracks, and alligator cracking that cause accidents and vehicle damage. Manual inspection is slow and resource-heavy, and there is no automated tool optimized to detect distress types unique to local roads under varying tropical weather and lighting conditions.

The objective was to train and deploy a custom computer vision model capable of detecting and classifying road damage in real time from mobile or dashcam footage.
tabs.process:
I structured and developed the machine learning pipeline:

  • Dataset Curation & Labeling: Collected and labeled thousands of road condition images via Roboflow, categorizing distress types (potholes, alligator cracking, and longitudinal cracks).
  • Model Training (YOLOv8): Fine-tuned a custom YOLOv8 object detection model using PyTorch, optimizing hyper-parameters like learning rate and anchor box sizes to match local damage dimensions.
  • Inference & Log Pipeline: Developed a real-time Python video processing pipeline using OpenCV to stream detections, output bounding boxes, and log coordinates.
tabs.outcome:
Key Insight: Tuning anchor sizes on small cracks improved detection recall by 22% compared to standard COCO weights, which frequently missed minor road cracks.

Outcome: Deployed a functional python-based computer vision prototype processing feeds at 30+ FPS, outputting damage classifications alongside simulated GPS coordinate mapping tags for easy logging.

tabs.problem:
Connecting customers with home maintenance technicians is often fragmented, relying on manual calls or overly complex platforms. Admin dispatchers lacked real-time visibility into technician workloads and schedules, while older or less tech-savvy customers struggled with standard web booking interfaces.

The objective was to build a secure, end-to-end service marketplace platform serving three distinct user roles: customer, technician, and admin.
tabs.process:
I designed and implemented the full system architecture from scratch:

  • Database Architecture (MySQL): Implemented a relational schema with 7 tables and proper foreign-key constraints to model the full service lifecycle—from initial booking through technician assignment to final payment and customer review.
  • Secure Backend (PHP): Built role-based authentication using hashed passwords and session-protected routing, using prepared statements (mysqli) throughout to robustly prevent SQL injection.
  • Accessible Design: Developed an accessibility-conscious customer booking interface featuring large targets, clear status badges, and secure multi-extension image uploads for troubleshooting.
tabs.outcome:
Key Insight: Simplicity and visibility are crucial for user adoption. Streamlining the booking flow with photo diagnosis reduced scheduling back-and-forth by letting technicians assess issues before dispatch.

Outcome: Deployed a secure, responsive prototype featuring real-time job status tracking (Pending → Assigned → In Progress → Completed) and a centralized admin dispatch dashboard prioritizing requests by urgency, significantly reducing booking-to-dispatch latency.

tabs.problem:
Executive leadership at a growing SaaS business lacked unified visibility into critical revenue metrics: rolling Monthly Recurring Revenue (MRR), Customer Acquisition Cost (CAC), and multi-month cohort retention, resulting in misaligned sales budgets.

Reporting was generated manually every week via spreadsheet merges, leading to frequent data quality errors.
tabs.process:
I built an automated BI architecture:

  • Data Warehouse Modeling: Designed a star-schema database model in SQL Server, unifying transactional data from Stripe, CRM histories from Salesforce, and web traffic logs.
  • DAX Engineering: Wrote advanced DAX measures in Power BI Desktop to calculate complex moving averages, active subscriber counts, and month-over-month MRR changes.
  • Visual Design: Created a clean interactive executive layout with robust tooltips, drill-through paths, and metric glossary cards.
tabs.outcome:
Key Insight: Discovered a 30% increase in CAC on search advertisements that yielded 40% lower retention cohorts. Conversely, developer webinars generated high LTV cohorts with low CAC.

Outcome: Replaced spreadsheets with real-time reporting. Armed with these insights, the CMO reallocated $50,000 of marketing spend away from low-performing ads, reducing overall CAC by 12%.

contact.heading: Let's discuss your next data project
contact.description: I'm currently open to analytics consultations, contract roles, and full-time analyst opportunities. If you want to convert dashboards from static sheets, write optimized SQL pipelines, or query insights out of logs, drop a message.
